steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice emphasizes a similarity between the Philippines and Liechtenstein. The notes show that in both countries the capital is not the most populous city — Manila is the capital but Quezon City is largest; Vaduz is the capital but Schaan is largest. A strong answer names both countries and states this shared property in a single, parallel sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘In both the Philippines and Liechtenstein, the capital is not the country’s most populous city’: States the shared property directly and covers both countries equally. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘the most populous city in the Philippines is Quezon City… in Liechtenstein is Schaan’: Lists the largest cities of both countries but never says how that relates to each capital, so no similarity is made explicit. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The most populous city in the Philippines is Quezon City, but the country’s capital is Manila’: Covers only the Philippines; Liechtenstein is not mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The capital of the Philippines is Manila; the capital of Liechtenstein is Vaduz’: Lists the capitals of both countries but omits the key fact about population, so no meaningful similarity is emphasized. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
